titleStmt.title: SII 20.136: original edition by G. S. Gai
No. 136. (B.K. No. 45 of 1936-37.) KAḌLĒVĀḌ, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.hi: Slab lying in front of the temple of Svāmiliṅga.

summary.p: This damaged inscription is dated in the Tribhuvanamalladēva year 3, Bahudhānya, Āśvija, śu. Pūrṇimā, Friday, lunar eclipse. The details correspond to A.D. 1158, October 9, which was a Thursday and not Friday as stated in the record. There was a lunar eclipse on that day.
1. The stone is chipped off after this.

The inscription seems to record a gift to the temple of Svayambhū-Sōmanāthadēva of Kaḍalevāḍa situated in Kummasi-30. Mentions [Rāma]dēvayya-nāyaka, the suṅkaverggaḍe of Tarddāvaḍi-1,000 province, Dāsimayya-nāyaka and taḷara-heggaḍe Nandimarasa. Also refers to a Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara (name lost) who is described as the ‘lord of Tagarapura’.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti sumadhigatapaṁcamahāśabda mahārājādhirāja tribhuvanama
lladēvavarśada 3 bahudhānya saṁvatsarada Āśvayuja suddha pu(ṁ)ṇnami su
kravārada sōmagrahaṇada parbba nimittavāgi śrī mattarddavā
rada suṁkaverggaḍe daṁḍanāyaka rāmadēvayya nāyaka
ruṁ dāsimayyanāyakaruṁ taḷara heggaḍe naṁdimarasanuṁ
ku(ṁ)mmasi mūvattaṟa baḷiya bāḍaṁ kaḍalevāḍada śrī svayaṁ
bhu sōmanātha da perjjuṁka vaḍḍarāvuḷada
worn out
samadhigatapaṁcamahāśabda mahāmaṁḍaḷēśvaraṁ tagaranagarādhīśvaraṁ
paraba prasūtaṁ sauryya
kadana pracaṁḍa vidagdhamugdhāṁganāhṛdayakusmakōda
nidānaṁ
ya manneya
maṁneyapannegavainatēya
nāmādisamastaprasastisahitaṁ śrīmadda
rasaru kummasigeya
kaḍelevāḍada śrīsvayaṁbhu sōmanātha
sāsirvvara The continuation is very badly damaged.
Digital edition of SII 20.136 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
